risk_if_not_implemented: Without Unattended End-User Equipment, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Physical Access Control
compensating_control_1.description: Physical access control mechanisms exist to enforce physical access authorizations for all physical access points (including designated entry/exit points) to facilities (excluding those areas within the facility officially designated as publicly accessible).
compensating_control_1.justification: Physical Access Control (PES-03) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Unattended End-User Equipment (AST-06) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Session Lock
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to initiate a session lock after an organization-defined time period of inactivity, or upon receiving a request from a user and retain the session lock until the user reestablishes access using established identification and authentication methods.
compensating_control_2.justification: Session Lock (IAC-24) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Unattended End-User Equipment (AST-06) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
